summary: Israeli warplanes carried out fresh airstrikes in southern Lebanon and Hezbollah drone activity struck civilian areas in Shomera and Metula. Iran issued multiple NOTAMs restricting Tehran FIR airspace and continues diplomatic talks with the United States in Doha. US political debate over a potential Iran deal intensifies while Israeli officials affirm no equipment shortages and ongoing operations against Hezbollah.
key_points: Israeli warplanes conducted fresh airstrikes on southern Lebanon (Source: @presstv/191322).
A hostile aircraft was intercepted over the western Galilee region (Source: @pkpoi/142193).
Hezbollah drone struck Shomera, causing a fire but no casualties (Source: @inon_yttach/8275).
Hezbollah drone hit Metula, prompting Israeli alerts (Source: @yediotnews25/129804).
Israel announced it is at war with Hezbollah and will increase strike intensity (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3022).
Iran issued NOTAMs restricting Tehran FIR airspace up to 20,000 ft through July 22 (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3015).
US‑Iran negotiations are ongoing with talks in Doha and mixed political reactions in the US (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3018).

sections.executive_summary: Israeli warplanes launched fresh airstrikes in southern Lebanon, marking a significant escalation on the northern front. Hezbollah drone attacks have struck civilian areas in Shomera and Metula, prompting heightened alerts. Iran’s airspace restrictions and ongoing US‑Iran diplomatic talks add further complexity to the regional security environment.

points: Iran issued multiple NOTAMs over Tehran FIR, activating restricted airspace zones and warning of gun firing exercises up to 20,000 ft, effective from May 30 through July 22 (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3015).
Iranian officials reported the reopening of internet access after an 87‑day outage, indicating internal policy shifts (Source: @asafroz/49494).
Top Iranian negotiators Qalibaf and FM Araqchi met in Doha with Qatar’s PM to discuss a potential US‑Iran deal (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3018).
US political criticism of a prospective Iran deal intensified, with Republicans questioning terms (Source: @presstv/191345).
Iranian state media noted that the supreme leader remains in a highly concealed location with limited external communication (Source: @VahidOnline/75693).
Israeli warplanes conducted fresh airstrikes on southern Lebanon, continuing operations against Hezbollah positions (Source: @presstv/191322).
The IDF reported activation of alerts for hostile aircraft over Hanita, with investigations ongoing (Source: @idf_telegram/23851).
Israel’s northern command chief Rafi Milu‘ah warned that Hezbollah deliberately intensified attacks on civilian populations in Metula and Shomera (Source: @yediotnews25/129804).
The IDF spokesperson emphasized there is no shortage of equipment and that procurement of defensive networks continues for forces in the north (Source: @yediotnews25/129805).
Prime Minister Netanyahu declared Israel is “at war with Hezbollah” and ordered increased strike intensity, citing drone threats (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3022).
IDF announced attacks on Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon, including in the town of Sour (Source: @idf_telegram/23856).
Six Palestinians were killed and eight injured by Israeli fire across the Gaza Strip, with reports of ongoing ceasefire violations (Source: @presstv/191352).
A Palestinian prisoner, Joudat Wael Omran, was released after two years in poor condition (Source: @presstv/191354).
Israeli airstrikes targeted southern Lebanon, focusing on Hezbollah positions (Source: @presstv/191322).
Hezbollah is reported to be firing drones, including fiber‑optic variants, prompting Israeli counter‑measures (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3022).
IDF operations have struck Hezbollah infrastructure in the town of Sour and other locations in southern Lebanon (Source: @idf_telegram/23856).
Six targets were attacked in the Lebanese town of Moshgara, according to security reports (Source: @New_security8200/199865).

key_developments: Israeli warplanes conducted fresh airstrikes on southern Lebanon, targeting Hezbollah positions (Source: @presstv/191322).
Hezbollah drone attacks struck Shomera, causing a fire but no casualties (Source: @inon_yttach/8275).
Hezbollah drone hit Metula, prompting Israeli alerts and statements of intensified conflict (Source: @yediotnews25/129804).
Iran issued NOTAMs restricting Tehran FIR airspace up to 20,000 ft through July 22 (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3015).
Iranian negotiators met in Doha with Qatar’s PM to discuss a potential US‑Iran agreement (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3018).
US political debate over a prospective Iran deal intensified, with Republican criticism reported (Source: @presstv/191345).
Prime Minister Netanyahu declared Israel is at war with Hezbollah and ordered increased strike intensity (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3022).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah’s use of explosive drones against civilian areas in Shomera and Metula demonstrates an escalating low‑altitude threat to northern Israeli communities, prompting heightened alerts and counter‑drone measures. Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon indicate a sustained campaign to degrade Hezbollah infrastructure, while Iran’s airspace restrictions could affect regional aviation and signal preparation for broader military activities. The convergence of diplomatic negotiations and internal political pressures in the US and Iran adds uncertainty to the conflict’s trajectory.
sections.regional_response: Israel’s leadership has publicly affirmed no equipment shortages and a commitment to intensify operations against Hezbollah, reflecting a unified military stance. Iran’s diplomatic outreach in Doha, coupled with internal policy changes such as reopening internet access, suggests a dual approach of negotiation and readiness. US political factions are debating the terms of any Iran deal, influencing the pace and scope of diplomatic engagement.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah drone deployments should focus on fiber‑optic and explosive variants, with particular attention to launch sites near the Lebanon‑Israel border. Iranian NOTAMs indicate possible training or weapons testing in the Tehran FIR, warranting air surveillance. Continued analysis of US‑Iran diplomatic channels is essential, as shifts in negotiation dynamics could rapidly alter regional security calculations.
